title: Day 4 · Diff gate + read-before-edit before any write
task: Use file_write to create hello.txt with one line 'hello from agent'

card.label: Use file_write to create hello.txt with 'hello from agent'

ledger.delta: messages += user('Use file_write to create hello.txt...')

frames.caption: CLI parses the prompt and builds ToolContext. The read_state slot Day 3 planted finally matters today — it's about to be consulted before any byte hits disk.

messages.content: Use file_write to create hello.txt with one line 'hello from agent'

frames.caption: The model sees file_write freshly registered at the end of default_tools and calls it. Notice file_write only has file_path / content — every safety policy (read-before-edit, mtime conflict, diff preview, y/N confirm) stays hidden from the model.

messages.content: Use file_write to create hello.txt with one line 'hello from agent'

gate.rule: Day 4 signature: resolve_in_cwd -> ensure_read_before_edit + check_mtime_conflict -> render_diff -> typer.confirm y/N

frames.caption: agent.py intercepts file_write / file_edit. On the new-file path: cwd lock passes; file doesn't exist so read-before-edit + mtime checks short-circuit; render_diff colors the '+hello from agent' line; typer.confirm prints 'Apply this edit to hello.txt? [y/N]' and waits for you. This is Day 4's signature frame — the model proposes, the harness decides.

messages.content: Use file_write to create hello.txt with one line 'hello from agent'

ledger.delta: path.exists()=False skips the backup branch; path.write_text(...); ctx.read_state.record(hello.txt)

frames.caption: You press y, control returns to tools.run. file_write sees path.exists()=False, so the whole backup branch is skipped — there's no old content to snapshot for a brand-new file. Then path.write_text lands the bytes, and ctx.read_state.record(hello.txt, content) stores (mtime_ns, char_count) so any subsequent file_edit on this file sails through ensure_read_before_edit.

messages.content: Use file_write to create hello.txt with one line 'hello from agent'

frames.caption: Same shape as Day 3: assistant's tool_use as one message, then all tool_result blocks in one user message. The new wrinkle today is the streaming emit — diff and confirm prompts get printed in the real order alongside tool_call / observation, instead of being dumped at the end.

messages.content: Use file_write to create hello.txt with one line 'hello from agent'

card.label: final: Created hello.txt with 'hello from agent'.

frames.caption: Second request. The model reads 'Wrote 16 chars to hello.txt' in the tool_result, concludes the write succeeded, and returns plain text with stop_reason=end_turn. The for-loop exits here.

messages.content: Use file_write to create hello.txt with one line 'hello from agent'

modelResponse.text: Created hello.txt with the content 'hello from agent'.

frames.caption: emit already streamed tool_call / diff / observation / final to the terminal in real order. ReadFileState now holds an entry for hello.txt — a follow-up file_edit on this file would clear ensure_read_before_edit without needing a fresh read_file.

messages.content: Use file_write to create hello.txt with one line 'hello from agent'

modelResponse.text: Created hello.txt with the content 'hello from agent'.
